title: Graduate School

Graduate School

Quick Solutions

Okan Homepage

Education

Academic Units

Graduate School

Quick Solutions

MENU

QUICK SOLUTIONS

QUESTIONS

ANSWERS

Where can I access the course schedule?

After your course registration is approved, you can view   the course schedule in the “Course Schedule” menu on the Student Information   System (SIS). Additionally, course schedules are published at the beginning   of each semester in the Announcements section of the Graduate School website.

How can I register for courses?

During the “course selection dates” indicated in the   academic calendar, select your courses through the Student Information System   (OIS) and submit them for your advisor’s approval (don’t forget to submit for   approval). Once approved by your advisor, you can finalize your course   registration.

My academic advisor is not appearing in the OIS. What   should I do?

You need to contact the Graduate School Academic   Coordination Unit.

Email:

akademikkoordinasyon@okan.edu.tr

I missed the course registration period. How can I   register for courses?

Students who could not register through the SIS during the   course registration period can apply to the Graduate School Secretariat by   filling out the

“Manual Course Registration   Form”

available on our website. Please note that students who   do not register on time risk being unable to attend classes. Therefore,   course registration should be completed at the beginning of the semester.

I have registered for courses, but they are still not   approved or have been rejected. What can I do?

You can contact your academic advisor listed in the   system.

I want to drop a course I selected and choose another one.

Students can make changes to their registered courses   during the dates specified in the academic calendar. If you want to change   your course selection during the add-drop period, use the

“Course Registration Change Request”

button on the course registration screen to modify your courses and submit   them again for your advisor’s approval.

How can I attend online courses?

Students can access online courses through the

O’Learn Education Platform

. Login to   O’Learn is done using your SIS username and password. A user guide is   available in the system.

My courses appear as in-person. How can I attend them?

For in-person courses, students need to go to the campus   where the course is held. Information about the campus and classroom for each   course is available in the

Course Schedule

menu on the OIS.

How can I see my courses on O’Learn?

Registered courses are listed under the

“Courses”

section. Enter the   course and join the class through the

“Class Collab”

section. A user guide is available in the system.

Can I rewatch my online courses?

You can rewatch your courses during the semester if they   have been recorded by the instructor. The recordings are available in the

Recordings

tab under the

Class Collab

section in the   system.

Where can I view my exam results?

You can view your exam results in the

Exam Results

section under   the

Documents

menu in the Student   Information System (SIS). Midterm and final exam results are announced by the   instructor no later than one week after the exam period. If you cannot view   your exam results, you can contact the instructor of the relevant course.

Why can’t I access the Student Information System (OIS)?

At our university, the Financial Affairs system and the   Student Information System (SIS) work together. If a student has overdue   payments and/or has not completed the financial record for the relevant term,   access to the SIS is blocked. If you cannot access the system for a different   reason, you should contact the

Student Affairs Unit

.

When will my system access be restored?

The system will be reopened as soon as the payment or   financial registration issue is resolved. Payments made to the accounts are   processed in the accounting system the following day. The system will not be   reopened until it is processed in the accounting system.

How can I access the Academic Calendar?

You can access it on the

Graduate   School

website.

Is an Academic Advisor different from a Thesis/Project   Advisor?

The

Academic Advisor

is the head/coordinator of the program you are enrolled in and provides   guidance for all academic processes and procedures.

The

Thesis/Project Advisor

is the   faculty member assigned by the program head to supervise your thesis or   project.

What is the completion period for a thesis-based master’s   program?

According to the

Istanbul Okan University   Graduate Education and Training Regulations

, the duration of   a thesis-based master’s program, excluding the time spent in scientific   preparation, starts from the semester in which the courses for the registered   program are offered. It consists of

four semesters

regardless of whether the student registers each semester, and the program   must be completed within a

maximum of six semesters

.

What is the completion period for a non-thesis master’s   program?

According to the

Istanbul Okan University   Graduate Education and Training Regulations

, the completion   period for a non-thesis master’s program, excluding the time spent in   scientific preparation, starts from the semester in which the courses for the   registered program are offered. The program must be completed in a

minimum of two semesters and a maximum of   three semesters

, regardless of whether the student registers   each semester. Students who fail or do not complete the program within this   period will be

disenrolled from the   University

..

What is the completion period for a doctoral program?

According to the

Istanbul Okan University   Graduate Education and Training Regulations

, for students   admitted with a thesis-based master’s degree (excluding time spent in   scientific preparation), the doctoral program starts from the semester in   which the courses for the registered program are offered. The program   consists of

eight semesters

, regardless   of whether the student registers each semester, and the

maximum completion period is twelve semesters

.

I don’t know how to write my thesis/final project. Where   can I access the guide?

You can access the guide on the

Graduate School

homepage   under the page titled

“

Thesis/Project Writing Guidelines

.

”
